'''Ben Harris''' (born 24 September 1983) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s. He became the first player in the history of the sport to have won grand finals in each hemisphere in consecutive seasons. Harris played in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], with whom he won the [[2004 NRL season|2004 premiership]], and the [[North Queensland Cowboys]]. He also played in England for the [[Bradford Bulls]], with whom he won 2005's [[Super League X]]. He played primarily as a {{rlp|ce}}.

==Canterbury-Bankstown==
Of [[English people|English]] descent and an Old Bar Pirates junior, Harris made his NRL début for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in 2002 at the age of 18.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/7992217.bulls-get-their-man/ | title=Bulls get their man }}</ref> He played in 42 first-grade matches for the club, including as the starting centre for their [[2004 NRL Grand Final]] victory over cross-city rivals, the [[Sydney Roosters]].

==Bradford Bulls==
Harris joined English club the [[Bradford Bulls]] in May 2005.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/harris-arrival-gives-bulls-a-timely-charge-491623.html|title=Harris' arrival gives Bulls a timely charge|last=Hadfield|first=Dave|date=22 May 2005|work=[[The Independent]]|accessdate=15 October 2011}}</ref> The Bradford Bulls won the [[Super League]] premiership in his first season there, and he started at centre in the [[2005 Super League Grand Final]] win over the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. He thus won premierships in each hemisphere over consecutive seasons, becoming the first player in the history of rugby league to do so.<ref name="BBC">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/bradford/6902140.stm|title=Centre Harris ends Bradford stay|date=17 July 2007|publisher=[[BBC Sport]]|access-date=15 October 2011}}</ref>
As [[Super League]] champions Bradford faced [[National Rugby League]] premiers the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[2006 World Club Challenge]]. Harris played on the wing in the Bradford Bulls' 30–10 victory.

==North Queensland Cowboys==
Harris returned to the NRL in 2008, signing for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] as a replacement for the retiring [[Paul Bowman (rugby league)|Paul Bowman]] in the centres.<ref name="BBC"/> In his first season for the Cowboys he was selected for the [[Country Origin]] team, his first and only representative football.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2008-04-28/stewart-cooper-lyon-withdraw-from-country/2417584|title=Stewart, Cooper, Lyon withdraw from Country|date=28 April 2008|publisher=[[ABC News (Australia)|ABC News]]|location=Australia|accessdate=15 October 2011}}</ref> Over time, he moved into the Cowboys' forward pack, playing in the [[rugby league positions#second row forward|second row]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/article/2009/02/12/38355_cowboys.html|title=Cowboy Harris settles into back-row role|last=Alston|first=Josh|date=12 February 2009|work=[[Townsville Bulletin]]|accessdate=15 October 2011}}</ref> He retired from professional football after injury plagued seasons in 2009 and 2010.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://resources.news.com.au/files/2011/01/09/1225984/374427-dtfile-nrl-preview.pdf|title=2011 NRL preview|publisher=News.com.au|accessdate=15 October 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/article/2010/06/16/146665_cowboys.html|title=Injuries forced Harris to consider retirement|last=Alston|first=Josh|date=16 June 2010|work=[[Townsville Bulletin]]|accessdate=15 October 2011}}</ref>
